title: Null Pointer Dereference
description: A pointer returned by a function may be NULL. Always check the return value before dereferencing.
good: char *p = malloc(10); if (p != NULL) { *p = 'a'; }

title: Resource Leak
description: Allocated resources such as memory, file handles, and sockets must be released on all paths including error paths.
bad: FILE *f = fopen('data.txt', 'r'); if (!f) return -1; /* f never closed */
good: FILE *f = fopen('data.txt', 'r'); if (!f) return -1; fread(buf, 1, sz, f); fclose(f);

title: Buffer Overflow via String Copy
description: Use size-bounded string functions (strncpy, strlcpy) instead of strcpy to prevent buffer overflows.
good: char buf[64]; strncpy(buf, user_input, sizeof(buf) - 1); buf[sizeof(buf) - 1] = '\0';

title: Buffer Overflow via String Concatenation
description: Use strncat instead of strcat to prevent buffer overflows during string concatenation.
good: char buf[64] = "hello"; strncat(buf, user_input, sizeof(buf) - strlen(buf) - 1);

title: Tainted Loop Bound
description: Never use externally-supplied data directly as a loop bound. Validate or clamp to a safe maximum first.
bad: for (int i = 0; i < user_count; i++) { buf[i] = 0; }
good: int n = (user_count < MAX_COUNT) ? user_count : MAX_COUNT; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { buf[i] = 0; }

title: Integer Overflow on Assignment
description: Check for integer overflow before performing arithmetic operations on signed integers.
good: if (a > 0 && b > INT_MAX - a) { /* handle overflow */ } else { int result = a + b; }

title: Double Free / Use After Free
description: Set pointer to NULL immediately after freeing. Never call free() on the same pointer twice.
good: free(ptr); ptr = NULL;

title: Uninitialized Stack Variable
description: All local variables must be explicitly initialized before use to avoid undefined behavior.
good: int status = 0; if (status == 0) { do_work(); }

title: Unchecked Return Value
description: The return value of functions that signal errors must always be checked.
good: if (remove('config.txt') != 0) { perror('remove failed'); }

title: Format String Vulnerability
description: Never pass user-controlled data directly as a printf-family format string.
good: printf("%s", user_message);

title: Tainted Memory Allocation Size
description: Never use unvalidated external input directly as an allocation size. Validate bounds first.
good: if (user_size == 0 || user_size > MAX_ALLOC) { return -1; } char *buf = malloc(user_size);

title: Use of Banned / Unsafe Functions
description: Avoid banned unsafe C functions: gets, sprintf, scanf without width, strcpy, strcat.
good: fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), stdin); snprintf(out, sizeof(out), fmt, arg);

title: Plaintext Password in Code or Log
description: Passwords must never be hardcoded, stored in plaintext, or written to logs.
good: /* Load password from secure vault; never log credentials */

title: TOCTOU Race Condition
description: Do not check a file's existence or permissions and then open it in separate steps. Use atomic operations.
good: fd = open(path, O_WRONLY); if (fd < 0) { perror('open'); return -1; }

title: Unsigned Integer Always-True Comparison
description: Comparing an unsigned integer with a negative constant is always true and indicates a logic error.
bad: unsigned int len = get_length(); if (len >= 0) { process(len); }
good: unsigned int len = get_length(); if (len > 0 && len < MAX_LEN) { process(len); }

title: Incorrect memset Size Argument
description: Always pass the size of the buffer (not the size of the pointer) to memset.
bad: char *buf = malloc(64); memset(buf, 0, sizeof(buf)); /* sizeof pointer, not buffer */
good: char *buf = malloc(64); if (buf) { memset(buf, 0, 64); }

title: Out-of-Bounds Array Access
description: Always validate array indices against the array size before accessing elements.
good: int arr[10]; if (index >= 0 && index < 10) { arr[index] = value; }

title: Unsafe Function Called from Signal Handler
description: Call only async-signal-safe functions from signal handlers. Avoid malloc, printf, and similar.
bad: void handler(int sig) { printf("Signal %d\n", sig); free(ptr); }
good: volatile sig_atomic_t g_signal_flag = 0; void handler(int sig) { g_signal_flag = sig; }

title: Integer Wraparound Before Size Check
description: Validate size arithmetic before using results in allocations to prevent wraparound attacks.
bad: size_t total = count * item_size; char *buf = malloc(total);
good: if (count != 0 && item_size > SIZE_MAX / count) { return -1; } size_t total = count * item_size; char *buf = malloc(total);

title: Use of Cryptographically Weak Random Number Generator
description: Do not use rand() or similar weak PRNGs for security-sensitive operations. Use a CSPRNG instead.
good: /* Use getrandom(), /dev/urandom, or a platform CSPRNG API for security tokens */
